title: Verify backend auth persistence and security
verify: Real Medusa/PostgreSQL flow persists Google/VK provider identity, customer, and link across a fresh process query.
First/repeat login, duplicate callback, same-email collision, missing email, invalid/replayed/expired state, and VK PKCE mismatch have expected atomic outcomes.
Session cookie creation/destruction, CORS, callback redirect, bounded rate limit, and restart logout behavior match contracts.
Provider doubles and evidence contain no live credentials, tokens, session IDs, raw IP, customer email, or production data.
evidence_required: Real Medusa/PostgreSQL acceptance output with redacted synthetic identifiers.
Security negative-case and evidence-secret scan output.
HUMAN_CHECKPOINT and rollback/recovery evidence required by T3 policy.
constraints: Exercise real Medusa Auth/Customer modules and local PostgreSQL, not an in-memory identity fake.
Use local provider doubles and synthetic identities only.
Do not change production auth behavior to make acceptance pass.
invariants: Durable identity/customer linkage survives process restart.
Session restart loss never deletes durable identity/customer/cart data.
No failure creates a partial link, duplicate customer, or usable session.
purpose: Prove the auth feature against its real Medusa/PostgreSQL security and persistence boundaries before browser acceptance.
success_outcome: Backend acceptance demonstrates durable, atomic, replay-safe identity/customer behavior and ephemeral safe sessions for both providers.
anti_goals: Do not use live providers or production secrets/data.
Do not replace real Medusa/PostgreSQL boundaries with mocks.
Do not add new auth behavior outside existing tasks.

stop_conditions: Acceptance cannot exercise real Auth/Customer PostgreSQL persistence.
Tests require live provider credentials or unredacted identity data.
A discovered implementation defect requires edits outside acceptance scope.
